description:
pyDREAM Poly LMA Hard-Sphere

Structure Factor:
Cluster-resolved hard-sphere Percus-Yevick structure factors are evaluated with one effective interaction radius per cluster profile and a shared interaction packing parameter phi_int.

Form Factor:
The solute form factor is a weighted mixture of the project's averaged cluster scattering profiles. SAXSShell generates weight parameters w0 ... wN-1 from the project component order, then this template normalizes those raw weights internally before applying the per-cluster hard-sphere structure factor.

Model Organization:
This template is intended to run through the production Prefit and DREAM workflows, not as a stand-alone example. The Prefit workflow supplies the generated component weights, solvent trace, averaged cluster intensities, and the computed cluster-geometry metadata array effective_radii. The same ordering is preserved in the DREAM runtime bundle.

Model Equation:
I_model(q) = scale * [ phi_solute * sum_i x_i I_i(q) S_HS(q; R_eff_i, phi_int) + solvent_scale * (1 - phi_solute) * I_solv(q) ] + offset

Internal Abundance Normalization:
x_i = w_i / sum_j w_j
w_i >= 0 for all i
sum_i x_i = 1 after normalization

Model Parameters:
w0 ... wN-1: Generated cluster-abundance coefficients aligned to the project component rows.
phi_solute: Solute volume fraction used to scale the cluster contribution relative to the solvent.
phi_int: Effective packing fraction used only inside the hard-sphere interaction model.
solvent_scale: Solvent trace multiplier applied before the final scale and offset.
scale: Global multiplicative scale factor applied to the combined model.
offset: Additive q-independent background term.
log_sigma: Natural logarithm of the Gaussian noise standard deviation used by the DREAM likelihood.

Cluster Geometry Metadata:
This template supports per-cluster geometry metadata. SAXSShell can compute centroid-aligned average semiaxes from each cluster folder, derive an equivalent-volume effective_radius, and recommend a structure-factor approximation of sphere or ellipsoid. The current production model uses the effective_radius mapping to build the per-cluster hard-sphere interaction term, while structure_factor_recommendation is retained for UI review and future template extensions.
